channel.title: networking on Memo Garcia
channel.description: Recent content in networking on Memo Garcia

item.title: Understanding Computer Networks by Analogy - Part 2 - Switches and Routers
item.description: Switches or “The floor concierge” Imagine that you want to send a package from room 69 to room 62. In a typical building you cannot go to your neighbor and give them the package, it’s rude, you need to do it through the floor concierge. The concierge or switch has a table of everyone’s door numbers: | Floor | Room number | Door number | |-------|-------------|-------------| | 6 | 602 | 1 | | 6 | 609 | 1 | Remember that each room can have many doors.

item.title: Understanding Computer Networks by Analogy - Part 1 - Networks and subnets
item.description: Prologue There are many ways to learn something new and no approach is best. But, what works best for me is a combination of trial and error and learning by first principles. But, as someone once said: Knowledge is only valuable when it leads to taking action on an idea. And as long as you share that knowledge you are taking action on an idea. Just one big question is: how to share them?
